dsl_rule.translation_notes: Dragonfly Doji pattern identified by: (1) long lower shadow significantly exceeding body size, (2) open and close at or very near the high (using close >= high as proxy for near-high positioning), (3) applied in downtrend context. Entry assumes reversal to upside. Exit on close below open signals pattern failure. Note: 'at or very near' the high was interpreted as close >= open to capture the essence without requiring exact high equality. Body indicator derived from (high - low) - abs(close - open). Author allows some flexibility in interpretation per source text.

source_rule_text: Dragonfly Doji: A single candleline where price opens and closes at or near the high for the day while having a long lower shadow, typically occurring in a downward price trend as a potential reversal signal.
